quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They beat their wings in a figure-eight pattern up to 80 times per second, which allows them to hover perfectly in place and even fly upside down. These tiny acrobats need to eat about half their body weight in nectar every day to fuel their incredible flying abilities.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Garden Snails
short_read.teaser: Those slow-moving snails in your garden are actually quite remarkable creatures with tiny teeth and surprising abilities. Discover the hidden world of these gentle garden companions.

medium_read.title: Hungary's Beautiful Lake Balaton: The Heart of Summer
medium_read.teaser: Lake Balaton, known as the 'Hungarian Sea,' has been a beloved summer destination for generations. Explore the traditions, thermal waters, and timeless charm of Central Europe's largest lake.

long_read.title: The Fascinating History of Kitchen Gardens Through the Ages
long_read.teaser: From medieval monastery gardens to Victory Gardens, kitchen gardens have fed families and communities for centuries. This beautifully illustrated journey explores how growing our own food has shaped culture, health, and connection to the land.

port_angeles.title: Harbor Seal Pupping Season at Ediz Hook
port_angeles.description: June is prime time to spot harbor seal mothers and their adorable pups along Ediz Hook! These gentle marine mammals haul out on the beaches to rest and nurse their young. Remember to keep a respectful distance of at least 100 yards to avoid disturbing these precious families.

puzzle_1.question: A ruby-throated hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute when in flight.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Amazing! Yes, 1,200 beats per minute — that's about 20 times per second! These tiny hearts work incredibly hard.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute — that's faster than you can count!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: These tiny hearts are truly amazing — they beat about 1,200 times per minute during flight! That's what it takes to power such incredible flying abilities.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To put this in perspective, a human heart beats about 60-100 times per minute at rest. Hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of all animals, which is why they need to eat so frequently throughout the day.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed butterfly — such a lovely, musical word for such a beautiful creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound as delicate as the creature itself? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language, unrelated to most European languages — it's part of the Finno-Ugric language family. Many Hungarian words have a musical, flowing quality that reflects the culture's love of poetry and song.

puzzle_3.question: What plant did Native Americans of the Olympic Peninsula traditionally use to make waterproof baskets and hats?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Exactly right! Cedar bark was woven into beautiful, waterproof baskets, hats, and clothing. The cedar tree was called the 'Tree of Life' for its countless uses in coastal Native cultures.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: The answer is cedar bark — this remarkable material was woven into waterproof baskets, hats, and even clothing. Cedar was so important it was called the 'Tree of Life' by coastal tribes.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The indigenous peoples of the Olympic Peninsula, including the Klallam, Makah, and Quileute, developed sophisticated weaving techniques over thousands of years. Cedar bark could be split into fine strands and woven so tightly it would hold water.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
